Patrician Scion.name: Patrician Scion
Patrician Scion.description: You were born into one of the Twelve High Houses, your bloodline steeped in the craft of furies for generations. From childhood, you learned the language of numina, trained in the academies of your House, and groomed for power. You command respect through breeding and education—yet this comfort has left you softer than street-born, your body untested by true hardship.
Patrician Scion.quirk:
Narration guidance: Patrician Scions carry themselves with entitled assurance and polish. They speak in measured tones, expect deference as a birthright, and move with the studied grace of court training. Despite their refined bearing, they often betray surprise or discomfort when confronted with rough necessity or direct disrespect to their station.

Description: You are a child of the Aquilan heartland's highest bloodlines. Your family name carries weight in the Senate and temples; your education occurred behind walls of privilege and mystery. You were groomed from infancy—trained in letters, rhetoric, and the deep craft of numina-communion. Your body is unmarked by labor; your hands have never known callus. But your mind has been honed relentlessly, and the gift runs deep in your blood, waiting for the will and discipline to awaken its full power.

Reception: In the Aquilan heartland and among civilized centers, your bearing and accent command immediate deference—doors open, lesser folk step aside. In conquered provinces, your House affiliation may inspire resentment or suspicion. On frontier lands and among barbarian-touched peoples, your obvious privilege can mark you as an outsider—even a target.

Social Behavior: You naturally assume leadership and expect to be obeyed. You speak in considered phrases, pause before responding, and rarely raise your voice. You notice slight discourtesies immediately and register them as personal affronts. When uncertain, you default to protocol and precedent rather than improvisation.

Collegium Prodigy.name: Collegium Prodigy
Collegium Prodigy.description: The Academy at Aquila recognized your gift early and claimed you for the Collegium's halls. There, surrounded by the brightest minds in the realm, you studied the deep lore of the numina, learned the theory of furycraft, and mastered the subtle arts of binding and communion. Your education was rigorous and complete, your mind honed to a knife's edge.
Collegium Prodigy.quirk:
Narration guidance: Collegium Prodigies carry themselves with barely-contained intellectual fervor. They speak in careful, measured language—sometimes too precise, betraying their recent release from academic discipline. They tend to explain rather than command, reference authority rather than claim it, and show genuine surprise when practical concerns override pure theory.

Description: You were educated in the Collegium's highest chambers, where theory and practice interweave. You understand the furies in ways most will never grasp—their nature, their language, their binding patterns, the subtle mathematics of their communion. Your mind is a library of accumulated knowledge, and your connection to the numina runs deep and peculiarly precise. Yet you were trained in the safety of academic halls: you have read about danger rather than faced it, studied violence in texts rather than lived it. Your knowledge may outpace your instincts.

Reception: Scholars, magistrates, and learned folk respect your obvious erudition. Common soldiers and street-dwellers often view you with suspicious awe—or barely concealed contempt for your lack of practical hardness. In the provinces, your Collegium accent may mark you as an outsider or arrogant imposer of Aquilan doctrine.

Social Behavior: You often speak in careful, theoretical language even when simplicity would suffice. You reference authorities and precedent constantly. When faced with immediate practical problems, you pause—analyzing rather than reacting. You show discomfort with violence, unpredictability, and departure from established protocols.

Gutter-Born.name: Gutter-Born
Gutter-Born.description: You survived the capital's underbelly—the cramped tenements, the crooked alleys, the markets thick with cutpurses and con-artists. Down in the gutters, you learned what matters: speed, deception, reading people like merchants read ledgers, and knowing which direction to run. Your mind is sharp with the cunning of the street; your body is quick from years of ducking and weaving through impossible spaces.
Gutter-Born.quirk:
Narration guidance: Gutter-Born characters move with economical, darting grace—they are never fully still, always aware of exits and threats. They listen far more than they speak, and when they do speak, it's often in coded language or slang. They read other people with unsettling accuracy, anticipating needs and betrayals alike. They flinch at sudden loud noises and tend to position themselves at the edge of conversations, ready to flee.

Description: Born in shadow and hunger. You are quick—not just in body but in mind. You read intention before words are spoken, sense violence before it happens, know which smile hides a blade and which offer is genuine. Your body is lean and efficient, honed by years of tight spaces and constant vigilance. You move with the grace of someone who learned to dance between rooftops and through crowded markets to stay ahead of predators. Your mind is a trap of deception—you have survived by understanding what people want, what they will pay for, what they can be tricked into believing. You have no formal education, no patron, no inheritance. You have only yourself, your wits, your speed, and the hard-earned knowledge that trust is a luxury that costs lives.

Reception: In the capital's lower districts, you are known—possibly feared. Among merchants and street people, you have credibility born of survival. In noble houses and formal settings, your accent and bearing mark you immediately as lower-class; many will dismiss or condescend. On the frontier and in provincial towns, you are an outsider with visible street-scars, which can trigger suspicion or pity.

Social Behavior: You listen far more than you speak. You position yourself at the edge of conversations and groups, never fully relaxed, always aware of exits. When you do speak, you choose words carefully and often use street slang or coded language. You read people with disturbing accuracy—you know what they want before they state it, sense when they are about to lie or turn. You are respectful to those who have shown you kindness, wary of those with power, and ready to run at any sign of real danger.

Born Strong.name: Born Strong
Born Strong.description: Raw, primal strength flows through your frame. Your muscles are naturally powerful, your grip formidable, your capacity for physical labor almost inexhaustible. You were simply born with the gift of mighty Potentia—a foundation of strength that sets you apart from your peers.
Born Strong.quirk: Strength is your birthright. Carry the heavy loads, move the immovable, endure the grind. Your body answers to will alone.

Fleet-Footed.name: Fleet-Footed
Fleet-Footed.description: Your legs carry you with preternatural swiftness. Whether you are sprinting across a battlefield or dodging through crowded streets, your natural speed gives you a decisive advantage. Your body moves with the quickness of a hunting hawk—swift, precise, and almost impossible to catch.
Fleet-Footed.quirk: Speed is your second skin. You move faster, react quicker, slip past where others are trapped. The world moves slower for you.

Iron-Bodied.name: Iron-Bodied
Iron-Bodied.description: Your flesh is tough as leather, your bones dense as oak. You were born with an uncommon resilience to pain and hardship. Blows that would fell others merely bruise you; sickness takes a weaker hold; exhaustion comes later. Your body simply endures where others would break.
Iron-Bodied.quirk: Your body is iron. It bends rather than breaks, endures rather than falls. Pain is a familiar friend, not a master.

Keen-Minded.name: Keen-Minded
Keen-Minded.description: Your mind is sharp, quick to perceive, quick to understand. Patterns reveal themselves to you; logic flows naturally; memory is your reliable servant. You were born with an intellect that sets you apart—a natural gift for learning, understanding, and piercing through deception.
Keen-Minded.quirk: Your mind is a blade. Sharp, quick, relentless. You see what others miss; understand what others struggle with. Intelligence is your weapon.

Indomitable Will.name: Indomitable Will
Indomitable Will.description: Your spirit is unbreakable, your conviction immovable. You were born with the gift of Voluntas—an iron will that resists pressure, charm, and doubt. Others may falter in their resolve; you simply press forward, unyielding as stone.
Indomitable Will.quirk: Your will is law—unto yourself, at least. Others bend; you stand. Persuasion slides off you like rain from armor. You decide, and the world must accommodate.

Numen-Touched.name: Numen-Touched
Numen-Touched.description: The furies themselves seem to know you. Your connection to the numina runs deeper than most—an innate affinity that makes communion easier, binding more natural, and the language of the ancient spirits almost second nature. You are Numen-Touched, blessed or cursed by an uncommon bond to the furycraft itself.
Numen-Touched.quirk: The furies whisper to you. Your Affinitas is a open channel to the numina, and they answer more readily than they should. Are you their instrument, or are they yours?

Bonded to Terra.name: Bonded to Terra
Bonded to Terra.description: You have formed a covenant with Terra, the primal spirit of earth and stone. Through this bond, you command the deep power of mountains, the strength of bedrock, the unyielding force of the land itself. Terra answers to your will with the weight of ages, granting you communion with the bones of the world.
Bonded to Terra.quirk: Terra dwells within you. The earth answers your call. Stone becomes your ally; the ground itself rises to your defense. You are never lost on solid land—the earth itself guides your steps.

Bonded to Ventus.name: Bonded to Ventus
Bonded to Ventus.description: You have formed a covenant with Ventus, the wild spirit of wind and sky. Through this bond, you command the capricious power of the breeze, the swiftness of the gale, the freedom of the air itself. Ventus dances to your call, carrying your will on the wings of the wind.
Bonded to Ventus.quirk: Ventus sings through you. The air obeys your whisper; the wind carries your voice beyond ordinary distance. You move faster when the breeze is at your back; you read the sky's moods as easily as another reads a face.

Bonded to Ignis.name: Bonded to Ignis
Bonded to Ignis.description: You have formed a covenant with Ignis, the ferocious spirit of fire and flame. Through this bond, you command the raw, destructive power of inferno, the transformative heat of forge and pyre, the primal hunger of conflagration. Ignis burns within you, demanding satisfaction through heat and destruction.
Bonded to Ignis.quirk: Ignis roars inside your chest. Fire bows to your will; smoke carries your scent; heat radiates from your very presence. You thirst for battle, for intensity, for the purification of burning. Control the flame, or it will consume everything you love.

Bonded to Aqua.name: Bonded to Aqua
Bonded to Aqua.description: You have formed a covenant with Aqua, the flowing spirit of water and tides. Through this bond, you command the gentle and relentless power of rivers, the crushing might of floods, the soothing balm of rain. Aqua shapes and flows through your will, adapting to any need.
Bonded to Aqua.quirk: Aqua flows through your veins. Water answers your command; you move with liquid grace; you sense coming storms as you sense hunger. You are at peace near water; you are danger to those who face you in rain or river.

Bonded to Ferrum.name: Bonded to Ferrum
Bonded to Ferrum.description: You have formed a covenant with Ferrum, the metallic spirit of iron and steel. Through this bond, you command the forged power of weapons, the unbreakable strength of armor, the rigid discipline of metal itself. Ferrum sings in your blood, binding you to discipline and the warrior's path.
Bonded to Ferrum.quirk: Ferrum rings in your bones. Metal answers your summons; weapons grow sharper in your hands; armor strengthens around you. Your will is iron; your resolve, unbreakable steel. You are the weapon; the weapon is you.

Bonded to Silva.name: Bonded to Silva
Bonded to Silva.description: You have formed a covenant with Silva, the verdant spirit of wood and wild growth. Through this bond, you command the patient power of forests, the stubborn life-force of roots and branches, the unseen networks of the wild. Silva speaks to you in rustles and whispers, binding you to the green world.
Bonded to Silva.quirk: Silva whispers through every leaf and root. Plants respond to your presence; the forest itself becomes your ally; you read the life of the land as an open book. You are more yourself in wild places; civilization feels like a cage.

Weapon Drilled.name: Weapon Drilled
Weapon Drilled.description: Years of rigorous training have made you a master of the martial arts. Your hands know the weight of sword and spear; your stance is perfect; your strikes are precise and devastating. You have drilled with blade and hafted weapon until combat flows as naturally as breathing.
Weapon Drilled.quirk: The blade is your language. Your muscle remembers every lesson; your reflexes answer before thought. You are a weapon made flesh.

Shield Trained.name: Shield Trained
Shield Trained.description: You have learned the discipline and art of shield-craft. Your arm knows the shield's weight; your stance is solid; your defense is nearly impenetrable. You protect not just yourself, but those behind you—shield and patience are your greatest strengths.
Shield Trained.quirk: The shield is your fortress. You shelter others behind your wall; your defense is nearly unbreakable. Where you stand, allies are safe.

Marksman.name: Marksman
Marksman.description: Your eye is true, your hands are steady, your aim is deadly. Whether with bow, javelin, or sling, you have mastered the art of striking from a distance with preternatural accuracy. Your precision is legendary; your patience, unshakeable.
Marksman.quirk: Your eye is your weapon. From any distance, with any condition, your aim is true. You see the target before others see the need. Death at a distance is your craft.

Brawler.name: Brawler
Brawler.description: You have learned the art of unarmed combat—grappling, striking, the close-quarters dance of fist and foot. Your body is a weapon; your knowledge of pressure points and leverage is encyclopedic. In close combat, you are devastating without ever drawing a blade.
Brawler.quirk: Your fists are your swords. You move with a fighter's grace; your strikes are calculated and brutal. You need no weapon save yourself.

Field Tactician.name: Field Tactician
Field Tactician.description: You have studied the art of war—strategy, positioning, the subtle orchestration of forces. Your mind reads battlefields like others read maps. You understand where pressure should apply, where defenses crumble, and how a few can defeat many through superior positioning and timing.
Field Tactician.quirk: The battlefield is your canvas. You see not just what is, but what could be. Where others see chaos, you see the underlying pattern—and know how to exploit it. You think three moves ahead.

Battlefield Healer.name: Battlefield Healer
Battlefield Healer.description: You have learned the craft of tending wounds under fire. Your hands know which herbs mend which injuries; your mind remains calm under pressure. You can draw a soldier back from death's door, stitch wounds with precision, and restore the fallen to fighting strength. Healing is your sacred gift.
Battlefield Healer.quirk: Your hands mend what others break. Blood doesn't frighten you; suffering sharpens your focus. You are needed where death circles closest.

Silver Tongue.name: Silver Tongue
Silver Tongue.description: Your words are your weapon, and you have mastered the blade. Persuasion, deception, negotiation—you move through social situations with the grace of a dancer. Others believe what you say because you say it so convincingly; doors open; walls crumble under the weight of your words.
Silver Tongue.quirk: Words are your tool. You can charm a throne out of a lord or trick a fool into giving up his purse. You read desire in an opponent's face and exploit it ruthlessly. Trust you at your peril.

Wilderness-Wise.name: Wilderness-Wise
Wilderness-Wise.description: You have learned the secrets of surviving beyond civilization's reach. Tracking, hunting, finding water and shelter, reading weather and terrain—the wild is your home, and its language is your native tongue. Where others are lost, you are merely home.
Wilderness-Wise.quirk: The wild is written in your bones. You move through forest and mountain as easily as in your childhood home. The land speaks to you; you listen and obey.

Lithe and Limber.name: Lithe and Limber
Lithe and Limber.description: Your body moves with the grace of a acrobat—flexible, responsive, capable of feats of physical prowess that leave others breathless. Climbing, tumbling, balancing on impossible perches, moving through tight spaces—your body simply does what others think impossible. Athletics is your second nature.
Lithe and Limber.quirk: Your body is liquid. You move with impossible grace; you climb what cannot be climbed; you fit through spaces that shouldn't fit bodies. Your flexibility is your freedom.

Duelist.name: Duelist
Duelist.description: You have trained in the refined art of finesse-fighting, where precision and swift steel outmatch brute strength. Your stance is elegant, your movements economical and deadly. You favor weapons that reward grace and timing over raw power—blade-work that dances rather than thunders.
Duelist.quirk: Swift steel and sharper wit. Your blade finds gaps in armor; your footwork keeps you always one step ahead. You dance where others stumble; you strike where others merely swing.

Shadowfoot.name: Shadowfoot
Shadowfoot.description: You have mastered the art of moving unseen. Your steps fall silent; your presence fades into shadow and periphery. Whether slipping past sentries, stalking prey, or vanishing in plain sight, your training in the craft of stealth makes you nearly invisible to those who would mark your passing.
Shadowfoot.quirk: Silence is your ally. You move like smoke, unseen and unheard. The watchers overlook you; the hunters' eyes slide past. You are only noticed when you choose to be.

Light Fingers.name: Light Fingers
Light Fingers.description: Your hands are quick, precise, and practiced in the delicate art of separating others from their possessions. Locks yield to your touch; coins vanish from purses; guards lose their keys without noticing. Your dexterity and timing are legendary among those who practice such craft.
Light Fingers.quirk: What is theirs becomes yours. Your hands are faster than eyes can follow; your touch leaves no trace. A purse, a key, a secret note—whatever your fingers desire, they take.

Tumbler.name: Tumbler
Tumbler.description: You have trained your body for acrobatic excellence—flipping, rolling, vaulting, and contorting with practiced precision. Whether escaping danger, traversing difficult terrain, or simply impressing onlookers, your command of your own body is extraordinary. Movement itself is your art.
Tumbler.quirk: Your body bends and soars. You tumble from ledges that would break lesser folk; you flip between rooftops; you move in ways that defy prediction. Gravity is merely a suggestion to you.

Beast-Friend.name: Beast-Friend
Beast-Friend.description: Animals know you. Whether through patience, understanding, or some deeper communion, beasts respond to your presence with unusual docility. You can calm a frightened horse, read a dog's intention, or predict a wild predator's movement. The language of animals is written in your bones.
Beast-Friend.quirk: The beasts know you. They sense something kindred in you—predator knows predator, prey knows safety. They trust you as they trust few humans. Can you betray that trust?

Smith.name: Smith
Smith.description: You have learned the Roman craft of the smith—heating iron, shaping steel at the forge, understanding the marriage of hammer and flame. Whether repairing weapons or crafting tools, your hands know the transformation of raw metal into useful form. The smithy is your sanctuary.
Smith.quirk: Fire and hammer speak your language. You shape metal as a potter shapes clay; you coax iron into strength. The ring of your hammer-blows is your song.

Alchemist.name: Alchemist
Alchemist.description: You have studied the mysterious art of alchemical transmutation—brewing potions, extracting essences, combining rare substances to create powerful compounds. Your knowledge of herbs, minerals, and esoteric processes grants you the ability to conjure remarkable effects from mundane materials.
Alchemist.quirk: Transformation through understanding. You read the secrets of matter and coax them into new forms. Poison or panacea—the choice is yours. Do you control the alchemy, or does it control you?

Tailor.name: Tailor
Tailor.description: You have mastered the craft of cloth and needle. Whether mending armor, crafting fine garments, or creating ceremonial dress, your hands transform raw fabric into pieces of beauty and function. Your work is both practical and artistic—every stitch a testament to your skill.
Tailor.quirk: Thread and needle know your intent. You transform cloth into armor, rags into finery. Your work is both craft and art; necessity and beauty flow from your hands.

Builder.name: Builder
Builder.description: You have learned the Roman arts of construction—understanding load and balance, measuring and planning, coordinating labor to create lasting structures. From simple shelters to ambitious buildings, your knowledge of architecture and building craft marks you as invaluable wherever civilization expands.
Builder.quirk: Structures rise at your command. You see the skeleton within the vision; you know where stone should rest and where it would crumble. Your hands raise monuments to endurance.

Woodwright.name: Woodwright
Woodwright.description: You are a master craftsman of wood—selecting timber, understanding grain and strength, shaping it into furniture, weapons, structures, or art. Your intimate knowledge of wood's nature allows you to coax it into forms others thought impossible. Forests give their treasure to your hands.
Woodwright.quirk: Wood speaks to you. You read its grain as others read faces; you shape it as a sculptor shapes clay. Your work endures; your craft is immortal in oak and ash.

Cook.name: Cook
Cook.description: You have mastered the art of preparing food—understanding flavors, methods, and the transformation of raw ingredients into nourishing or delightful dishes. Whether feeding soldiers in the field or preparing feasts for nobles, your skill sustains bodies and delights palates. Hunger yields to your craft.
Cook.quirk: Your hands transform sustenance into art. You coax flavor from humble ingredients; you know which spice enhances and which kills. A good meal from your hands can change a mood or seal an alliance.

Miner.name: Miner
Miner.description: You have spent your years in the depths, extracting precious metals and stones from stone's heart. Your knowledge of mining—where to dig, how to read rock, when danger approaches—is hard-won through blood and callus. The earth's treasures yield slowly to those who know how to ask.
Miner.quirk: The earth's secrets are yours. You know stone's heart; you sense where metal sleeps and where danger lurks. Your hands are sculpted by picks and stone; your back bent by honest labor.

Mason.name: Mason
Mason.description: You have learned the craft of stonework—selecting stone, cutting it true, mortaring it into lasting structures. From aqueducts to temples, from fortifications to fine buildings, your knowledge of stone and its proper working has built the realm. Rome's walls know your hands.
Mason.quirk: Stone obeys your command. You see the hidden lines within raw rock; you know where it will cleave and where it will hold. Your work stands for centuries—monuments to your craft.

Goldsmith.name: Goldsmith
Goldsmith.description: You are a master of fine metalworking—shaping precious metals into objects of beauty and value. Your expertise in gold, silver, and other fine materials, combined with your artistic vision, allows you to create jewelry, ornaments, and treasures that command respect and wealth. Your craft is both practical and transcendent.
Goldsmith.quirk: Precious metals yield to your vision. You see the form within the bullion; you shape beauty from raw wealth. Your creations outlast empires, catching light and turning it into art.

Enchanter.name: Enchanter
Enchanter.description: You have learned the delicate art of imbuing objects with numen—binding spirits into items, weaving fury-craft into weapons and armor, awakening the latent power within mundane objects. Your understanding of the marriage between physical and spiritual makes you invaluable to those who wield furycraft.
Enchanter.quirk: Objects sing under your touch. You bind fury into steel; you awaken numina in ordinary things; you make the mundane magical. Every piece you craft carries a whisper of the sacred.

Loremaster.name: Loremaster
Loremaster.description: You have devoted yourself to the study of history, legend, and ancient wisdom. Through years of scholarship in libraries, temples, and the company of elders, you have amassed a deep knowledge of the empire's past, the tales of heroes long dead, and the secrets written in dust and parchment. Your memory is a living archive; your voice carries the weight of ages.
Loremaster.quirk: The past lives in your mind. You know the history of bloodlines, the genealogy of emperors, the forgotten names of ancient spirits. Others listen when you speak—for you carry within you the wisdom of centuries.

Performer.name: Performer
Performer.description: You have mastered the arts of entertainment—music, theater, dance, and the subtle craft of captivating an audience. Whether playing a lyre in a nobleman's hall or telling tales in a tavern, your presence commands attention and your skill stirs hearts. You understand rhythm, emotion, and the precise moment when a crowd holds its breath.
Performer.quirk: You are the stage, and the stage is you. Your voice carries power; your movements entrance; your art moves people to tears or joy. Through performance, you touch souls in ways mere words cannot reach.

Horseman.name: Horseman
Horseman.description: You have spent your life in the saddle, mastering the art of horsemanship—breaking wild mounts, understanding their nature, and becoming one with the beast beneath you. Whether charging into battle or navigating treacherous terrain, your bond with horses is intimate and profound. You move with your mount as if you were a single creature.
Horseman.quirk: Horse and rider are one when you mount. The beasts know your touch and respond to the slightest shift in your weight. You read their moods as easily as your own heartbeat; they would follow you into death.

Mariner.name: Mariner
Mariner.description: You have devoted your years to the sea—sailing rivers and seas alike, understanding wind and wave, reading the moods of water and sky. Your hands know rope and sail; your eyes read weather and current; your heart belongs to the restless water. Few lands know your feet; the waves are your true home.
Mariner.quirk: The sea calls to you always. You read the ocean's secrets in the shape of waves; you know which winds will come by the color of the sky. You navigate by stars and instinct alike. The water trusts you; you trust the water.

Investigator.name: Investigator
Investigator.description: You have honed your mind to pierce deception and unravel secrets. Whether examining a crime scene, questioning suspects, or tracking hidden truths, your keen eye catches details others overlook. You understand the threads of cause and effect, and you follow them relentlessly. In the empire's complex web of power and intrigue, your skill in investigation marks you as dangerous to those with secrets.
Investigator.quirk: You see what others miss. Your mind works backward from evidence to truth; your questions probe the weak points in any story. Nothing escapes your notice—not the tremor in a voice, not the inconsistency in testimony, not the absence of what should be present. You are a hound of truth.

Enforcer.name: Enforcer
Enforcer.description: You have learned the trade of dominance through presence and force of will. Whether serving the imperial authorities or private masters, you understand how to bend others to obedience through conviction and controlled intimidation. Your reputation precedes you; your words carry weight; your gaze carries menace. In Rome's hierarchies of power, you are the fist that ensures compliance.
Enforcer.quirk: People fear you. Your presence alone can silence a tavern; your words carry the weight of consequence. You move through crowds and they part before you. You understand the delicate art of suggestion and threat—when to whisper, when to raise your voice, when a single glance suffices to remind someone of their place.

Orator.name: Orator
Orator.description: You have trained yourself in the sacred art of rhetoric—the craft of moving crowds, commanding the Senate floor, and swaying tribunals with the force of your words alone. From childhood, you studied the great speeches of the past, learned to read the moods of assemblies, and mastered the delicate balance of persuasion and authority. Your voice carries the weight of conviction; your words reshape belief. In Rome's halls of power, you are the living embodiment of oratorical excellence.
Orator.quirk: Words are your greatest weapon. You have spent years mastering the rhythms of speech, the art of pausing for effect, the precise moment to raise your voice or lower it to a whisper. When you speak in the Senate, senators lean forward to listen. Before the mob, crowds fall silent to hear your words. You command the tribunal not through fear but through the sheer force of your eloquence. Your every phrase has been tested, refined, practiced until it strikes with the precision of a blade.

Arena-Bound.name: Arena-Bound
Arena-Bound.description: You were condemned to the sands of the Circus Aquilae, sold into bondage and forged by survival in the fighting pits. Every scar is a lesson in endurance; every victory, a breath closer to freedom. Your instincts are honed for combat; your will unbreakable despite the iron brand of slavery that marks you as property. In the arena, you learned that combat is truth—hesitation is death, mercy is weakness, and survival is everything. Freedom awaits, one duel at a time.
Arena-Bound.quirk:
Narration guidance: Arena-Bound characters move with coiled predatory grace even when still. They read combat situations with terrifying speed, and their stillness before action is eerie—like a blade sheathed. They speak sparingly, often in present tense about immediate threats. When triggered by violence or capture, they become someone else entirely: faster, colder, focused with an intensity that can be disturbing to witness.

Description: The sand is your home, though you never chose it. Your body bears the scars of a thousand contests—missing teeth, crooked fingers, thick whorls of scar tissue. You carry the iron brand of a condemned gladiator, a permanent mark of slavery visible to all. Your movements are controlled and economical, born from the necessity of conserving energy between bouts. You move with the grace of a predator who has killed to eat, starved to stay sharp. Your will was broken once—yes, broken completely—but when the pieces fused back together, they fused into something harder, stranger. You carry within you the hunger of a fighter who has glimpsed freedom through the crowd's roar and will not surrender that vision.

Reception: Common folk react with visceral awe—the arena is the only place lower classes can achieve fame and wealth, and the marked carry celebrity. Magistrates and elites regard you with a mix of entertainment value and unease—you are a tame weapon, but weapons turn. In conservative or civilized settings, your brand marks you as criminal property. Some will flinch; some will try to own you again.

Social Behavior: You observe much and speak little. When you do speak, you are precise—no wasted words. You have learned to read violence before it happens, to sense danger the way others sense weather. In conversation, you tend toward present tense and immediate concerns. You are respectful to those in authority (learned through brutal enforcement), but if threatened or cornered, something primal and dangerous surfaces instantly.

Aquilan.name: Aquilan
Aquilan.description: You are of the heartland—a citizen of the Imperium's core, raised in the shadow of the capital's temples and forums. Your bloodline is rooted in the soil where the empire's power concentrates, and you carry yourself with the civic pride of one who believes the center of the world radiates outward from Aquila itself. You are cultured in the furycraft, educated in the protocols of power, and accustomed to deference. Yet that same privilege can mark you as arrogant in the eyes of provincial peoples.
Aquilan.quirk:
Narration guidance: Aquilans carry themselves with quiet civic authority. They expect and receive deference in civilized settings. They speak of 'the Imperium' as though it were an extension of themselves. When encountering frontier peoples or provincial customs, they register them as charming but inferior, sometimes with barely concealed disdain. They move with the confidence of those who have never questioned their place in the hierarchy.

Description: You were born in or near the Aquilan heartland, in the web of cities and towns that radiate the empire's cultural power. Your family owns land, conducts trade, or holds position—you are no pauper. You grew up hearing stories of the empire's expansion, educated in the rhetoric of Aquilan civilization and superiority. Your manner is polished: you speak in measured tones, understand the protocols of formal speech, and move with the ease of someone who has never struggled for basics. The furycraft flows through your bloodline—whether strong or weak, you were raised to understand it, to respect it, to use it as a tool of power.

Reception: In the Aquilan heartland and major civilized centers, your origin commands automatic respect. Other Aquilans recognize you as one of their kind. In conquered provinces, your bearing may inspire resentment—you are seen as a representative of the occupation. On savage frontiers, you are viewed as a soft outsider, cultured but untested. Some provincial peoples resent what they see as arrogance; others seek to emulate your style as a path to advancement.

Social Behavior: You naturally assume leadership and expect to be heard. You speak with precision, using formal rhetoric when appropriate. You take pride in understanding complex social dynamics. You notice slight infractions of protocol and correct them—sometimes subtly, sometimes not. In the presence of those you judge inferior, you remain courteous but distant. When disrespected, you respond with cold dignity rather than anger.

Highlander.name: Highlander
Highlander.description: You hail from the mountain valleys and high plateaus where the empire's reach grows thin and ancient bloodlines run deep. Your people are stubborn, martial, and shaped by harsh terrain. You are accustomed to hardship and endurance; you know the value of honor and the weight of tradition. Though the Imperium claims your hills, your true loyalty runs to your kin and your mountains.
Highlander.quirk:
Narration guidance: Highlanders move with economic, powerful strides. They tend to be quiet and watchful, speaking only when necessary—but when they do speak, people listen. They read terrain and weather with ease, seem entirely comfortable in mountains or rough country, and can appear almost uncomfortable in flatlands or cities. They value straightforward honesty and can seem blunt or rude to more formal peoples. They are fiercely protective of those they consider kin or allies.

Description: You were born in the high valleys where winters bite and summers are short. Your people have lived in these hills for generations—before Rome came, during Rome's conquest, and carrying that history forward. Your bloodline is old hill-stock: Varran, Sabine, or similar hardy peoples whose names predate the empire. Your body is strong and lean, marked by hard labor and exposure. You move with the ease of one who has always lived in steep terrain, who knows how to read stone and slope, who can navigate fog and darkness. Your people value martial skill, straightforward honor, and practical wisdom. You were raised on stories of your ancestors, taught to ride and fight young, instructed in the protocols of your clan. The empire claims your hills and taxes your people, but your true loyalty remains tribal—to your house, your valley, your blood.

Reception: In the empire's heartland, you are regarded with respect tinged with caution—your people have a reputation for martial prowess and stubborn independence. Soldiers and garrison commanders treat you with wariness and courtesy. Civilized folk often see you as rustic but trustworthy. In provincial cities, you may be underestimated or stereotyped as rough. On the frontier, you are trusted more readily—your toughness is understood and valued.

Social Behavior: You speak less than you listen. You tend toward bluntness—you say what you mean, see little value in flowery language, and expect others to do the same. You are loyal to those you have chosen to trust, and that loyalty is absolute. You are uncomfortable with elaborate courtesies and formal rhetoric. You notice disrespect immediately and respond directly. When faced with injustice, you tend toward action rather than negotiation.

Northborn.name: Northborn
Northborn.description: You were raised on the bleeding northern frontier, where the empire's legions wage endless war against barbarian tribes and the land itself is hostile. Your childhood was shaped by conflict, scarcity, and the constant proximity of death. You are weathered beyond your years, touched by hardship and the wild in equal measure. Though the empire claims to civilize the north, the north has civilized you instead.
Northborn.quirk:
Narration guidance: Northborn characters carry themselves with visible wariness—they watch terrain and other people with equal intensity. They seem most comfortable in rough country or violent situations and can appear tense in peaceful civilized settings. They speak in clipped, practical language and have a dark, gallows humor about suffering. They tend toward paranoia about being outmaneuvered and watch for betrayal constantly. Despite their caution, they show surprising loyalty to those who have watched their back.

Description: You were born on the northern limes where the empire's eagles fly over bloodstained ground. Your childhood was not quiet—you learned to fight almost as soon as walk, learned to see strangers as potential enemies, learned that the line between survival and death is paper-thin. Your people—whether garrison soldiers, hardy settlers, or those descended from barbarian captives—taught you practical endurance. Your body is scarred, weathered by cold and wind. Your senses are sharp—you read weather before it arrives, sense danger the way others sense hunger. You are comfortable with violence, accustomed to scarcity, and unsurprised by betrayal. The north has marked you: you carry its hardness in your bones, its suspicion in your eyes.

Reception: Soldiers and garrison folk respect your toughness and reliability. In the Aquilan heartland, you are viewed as half-wild, interesting but somewhat frightening. Civilized folk often seem too soft and naive to you, and that judgment shows. In provincial cities, you are an outsider with visible scars and an accent marked by the frontier. Some see you as dangerous; others trust you precisely because you understand violence and survival. On the frontier itself, you are home.

Social Behavior: You are direct and pragmatic. You waste no time on pleasantries and expect others not to waste yours. You read violence coming before it happens and position yourself accordingly. You are suspicious of elaborate plans and social games—you prefer to know where you stand. You are intensely loyal to those who have fought beside you or proven their reliability. You are quick to anger at betrayal and slow to forgive it. In peaceful settings, you tend toward vigilance—you seem coiled, ready to fight.

Eastlander.name: Eastlander
Eastlander.description: You are descended from or born into the philosopher-cities of the eastern reaches, ancient places where learning and commerce flow like water. Your people are known for intellect, eloquence, and a merchant's gift for reading desire and opportunity. Yet that same sophistication and cosmopolitanism can brand you as a foreign schemer in the eyes of conservative, traditional Romans.
Eastlander.quirk:
Narration guidance: Eastlanders carry themselves with fluid grace and obvious comfort in social situations. They are naturally attuned to what others want and often anticipate needs before they are voiced. They speak easily and well, code-switching between formal and casual register. They seem at ease in cities and uncomfortable in wild places. They are naturally suspicious of direct violence as a solution and tend toward negotiation and manipulation instead. Despite their surface grace, they often carry hidden agendas or divided loyalties.

Description: You were born in one of the eastern cities—Antioch, Alexandria, Damascus, or their rivals—where the ancient world's learning concentrates. Your people have traded and philosophized for generations. Your family likely conducts trade, follows a learned profession, or holds minor position. You were educated in rhetoric, history, and the subtle arts of reading people and situations. Your manner is naturally gracious—you move with the ease of one who has always lived in cities, who understands that words are more powerful than swords, that desire is the currency that moves the world. You are familiar with multiple languages, understand the protocols of different peoples, and can adapt your presentation to suit your audience. The furycraft exists in your eastern cities but is woven differently—more intellectual, more negotiated with than commanded.

Reception: In other eastern cities and civilized centers, you are treated as a peer—another learned person, another civilized individual. In the Aquilan heartland, you are viewed with respect but also suspicion—your foreign accent, your easy confidence with languages, your tendency to negotiate rather than decree mark you as 'other.' Soldiers and frontier folk often regard you with wariness or contempt, seeing you as soft or untrustworthy. Some view your sophistication as proof of hidden agendas; others admire your intelligence. Conservative Romans often suspect your loyalty to the empire itself.

Social Behavior: You are naturally gracious and attuned to social dynamics. You speak easily and well, adjusting your language and approach to your audience. You are skilled at reading what others want and providing it—or suggesting they want what benefits you. You avoid direct conflict, preferring negotiation and subtle influence. You are comfortable with ambiguity and with maintaining multiple relationships or commitments. You notice social hierarchy immediately and position yourself accordingly. To those in power, you are deferential and helpful; to those beneath, you are courteous but distant.

Tharsi-blooded.name: Tharsi-blooded
Tharsi-blooded.description: Your blood is marked by Tharsis—the rival sea-empire to the empire's west, a power that Rome defeated but never fully conquered. You may be descended from Tharsi nobility, a subject of their hybrid rule, or bear the cultural marks of their influence. You carry an exotic legacy that marks you as simultaneously fascinating and suspect—admired for your foreign mystique, feared as potentially enemy-aligned, distrusted as the product of a rival power.
Tharsi-blooded.quirk:
Narration guidance: Tharsi-blooded characters move and speak with a distinctive style that marks them as foreign. They tend to be striking in appearance—different coloring, dress, bearing. They are comfortable with ritualism and symbolism in a way that can seem theatrical to Romans. They often carry obvious cultural markers: tattoos, amulets, specific ways of dressing or adorning themselves. They can switch between seeming mysterious and unknowable to being surprisingly open and warm. Beneath a graceful surface often runs a calculating awareness—they are navigating a world that sees them as inherently compromised.

Description: Your connection to Tharsis marks you. Perhaps your grandmother was Tharsi, perhaps your family fled Aquilan rule, perhaps your blood is mixed. Regardless, you carry the cultural and physical markers of a rival power—different features, different bearing, different knowledge of the world. The Tharsi sea-empire was a genuine threat, defeated and subdued but never fully absorbed. Your people practice different rites, follow different gods or spirits, understand power and furycraft through a different lens. Your appearance alone marks you: you might have darker skin, distinctive tattoos, clothing that marks your foreign origin, jewelry or adornments that proclaim your heritage. You are comfortable with symbolism, ritual, and a style of grace that Romans sometimes read as dangerous or seductive. You understand yourself as living between worlds—never fully trusted in the Imperium, but unable or unwilling to fully surrender your heritage.

Reception: Civilized Aquilans are often fascinated by your exotic nature, but this fascination is mixed with fear and distrust. Many Romans suspect hidden loyalty to Tharsis or hidden magical knowledge tied to their ancient blood-rites. Soldiers and frontier folk often regard you with suspicion—your foreignness is too visible, too marked. Some will assume you are a spy; others will try to use you as one. In very conservative settings, you may face outright hostility rooted in old wars. However, you also carry a mystique that attracts those interested in the unusual or the forbidden.

Social Behavior: You carry yourself with a distinctive grace and confidence that comes from belonging to a proud, ancient culture. You are often comfortable with ritual, ceremony, and the formal performance of respect—though your style may differ from Aquilan protocols. You read political situations quickly and position yourself carefully. You are skilled at seeming whatever others need you to be—mysterious, approachable, threatening, vulnerable—while maintaining an inner core of privacy. You are aware that you are watched and suspected, and you use that awareness strategically. You are loyal to those who treat you with genuine respect, but you rarely fully trust anyone from the dominant power.

Provincial.name: Provincial
Provincial.description: You are common stock—born to plebeian families in the empire's conquered provinces, where most of Rome's actual population lives. You are adaptable and resourceful, learned in the practical arts of survival and social navigation. You are socially invisible in many contexts, overlooked and underestimated. Yet that invisibility is also your strength—you understand how systems actually work in ways the privileged never learn.
Provincial.quirk:
Narration guidance: Provincial characters move through the world with an almost animal awareness of social hierarchies and power. They are naturally good at reading situations and people, at finding the path of least resistance. They often seem to fade into background—not through stealth but through a kind of social adaptation, a chameleon-like ability to blend. They speak with less polish than Aquilans, using local dialect and practical language. They are comfortable with ambiguity and moral compromise. Despite their social invisibility, they often carry surprising depths of knowledge and unsentimental wisdom.

Description: You were born in the provinces—one of the empire's many conquered territories where actual daily life happens far from the centers of power. Your family are farmers, craftspeople, merchants, soldiers, or some mixture thereof. You are not poor necessarily, but you are not privileged. Your education came from experience rather than tutors: you learned to read situations, to know which officials could be swayed and which were immovable, to understand the invisible rules that govern how the conquered live under the conquerors. You are adaptable—you learned early that survival requires flexibility, that the appearance of compliance can hide all manner of actual resistance. You understand how systems work because you navigate them daily. Your accent marks you as provincial; your hands often bear the marks of labor; your manner is direct and practical rather than polished. You are socially invisible in many contexts—easily overlooked, underestimated, dismissed. This invisibility is your greatest asset.

Reception: Aquilans often regard you as charming rustic or simply ignore you entirely—you are beneath their notice unless you become obviously wealthy or notable. Other provincials recognize you as one of their own. Soldiers stationed in the provinces understand your pragmatism and often work with you without condescension. Foreign peoples often regard you as a representative of the occupation, but your lack of obvious elite markers can make them treat you more as fellow commoner than oppressor. Conservative authorities often trust you precisely because you appear harmless.

Social Behavior: You are naturally good at reading what people want and understanding how to work within existing systems. You move through the world with practical awareness rather than ambition. You are skilled at seeming less threatening than you are, less knowledgeable than you actually are. You are comfortable with moral ambiguity—you understand that survival sometimes requires compromise. You are loyal to your actual community (family, village, guild) rather than to abstract ideals. You are suspicious of sudden fortune or obvious paths of advancement—if it seems too good, you trust your instincts that something is wrong.

Company Rank: D.name: Company Rank: D
Company Rank: D.description:
Oratory: +2

D-Rank Free Companion. You begin registered with the Free Companies, a fresh bronze charter-tag at your throat. You are authorized for the lowest rung of the contract ladder -- escort runs, pest-culling, minor errands in settled country -- with a reputation to match: none yet earned.
Company Rank: D.quirk: This character is a freshly enrolled D-Rank of the Free Companies, carrying a plain bronze charter-tag with no name yet behind it. Narrate seasoned companions sizing them up by the bronze and giving their words little weight, hall-clerks explaining procedure slowly, innkeepers steering them to the cheap cots, House guards waving them past without stepping aside, and local toughs not bothering to lower their voices. To most they are a hopeful with a contract-chit, not a name -- and the work the boards offer them should reflect that: strayed livestock, escort duty, rats in a granary. They are recognized this way only while openly displaying the bronze tag; kept out of sight, it leaves them to pass for an ordinary traveler, drawing none of these reactions.

Company Rank: C.name: Company Rank: C
Company Rank: C.description:
Oratory: +4

C-Rank Free Companion. You carry the silver tag, trusted by the Companies to break organized banditry, hunt dangerous beasts, and settle straightforward furycraft disturbances -- and to work alone, anywhere the charter runs, far from any home territory.
Company Rank: C.quirk: This character is a C-Rank of the Free Companies with real work behind the title and a silver tag to show it, trusted to operate alone anywhere the charter runs. Narrate guards returning their nod and taking their reports at face value, innkeepers offering a decent room unasked, and veteran companions treating them as a peer on a job rather than ballast -- no one checks their gear before working alongside them. Merchants quote fair prices and minor officials answer their questions. Those who live outside the law notice them too, not with fear but with the wary arithmetic of whether they are worth the trouble of crossing. They are recognized this way only while openly displaying the silver tag; kept out of sight, it leaves them to pass for an ordinary traveler, drawing none of these reactions.

Company Rank: B.name: Company Rank: B
Company Rank: B.description:
Oratory: +6

B-Rank Free Companion. The steel tag marks a proven professional, set to serious threats -- monster incursions, regional feuds, complex furycraft anomalies -- and eligible for the special charges the Chapterhouse hands out by name.
Company Rank: B.quirk: This character is a B-Rank of the Free Companies, a proven professional whose name travels on the contract boards ahead of them; the Chapterhouse knows it. Narrate guards stepping aside unasked, officials granting meetings on short notice, younger companions recognizing the name from dispatches, and merchants setting fair prices without haggling. Regional authorities request them by name when something has gone badly wrong, and the dangerous element -- bandit captains, fences, the quiet operators in every city -- treats them as a factor to plan around rather than brush aside. Play them as someone past proving they belong, now choosing which demands on their attention deserve it. They are recognized this way only while openly displaying the steel tag; kept out of sight, it leaves them to pass for an ordinary traveler, drawing none of these reactions.

Company Rank: A.name: Company Rank: A
Company Rank: A.description:
Oratory: +8

A-Rank Free Companion. The gold tag is an elite charge, deployed to catastrophes, politically poisoned missions, and threats that could unmake a whole region. It carries something near diplomatic immunity across most borders -- earned in service, never handed across a desk.
Company Rank: A.quirk: This character is an A-Rank of the Free Companies, and the gold tag opens doors rank alone cannot. Regional authorities seek their counsel before making decisions. Commanders share intelligence they should not. House officials extend courtesies normally reserved for state envoys. Their arrival in a city changes the political arithmetic, and powerful people adjust their plans around the simple fact of their presence. They are recognized this way only while openly displaying the gold tag; kept out of sight, it leaves them to pass for an ordinary traveler, drawing none of these reactions.

Company Rank: S.name: Company Rank: S
Company Rank: S.description:
Oratory: +10

S-Rank Free Companion. An unofficial mark reserved for the few whose deeds outrun all classification -- called upon for catastrophes that threaten the world itself, and granted an authority no charter dares put in writing.
Company Rank: S.quirk: This character is an S-Rank of the Free Companies, their name known across the realm. Rulers request their presence by letter. Generals defer to their judgment in the field. The tag they carry is less identification than formality -- everyone already knows who they are. Whole regions remember what they did for them, and doors that have never opened for anyone open for them. Narrate the world rearranging itself, quietly, around the fact that they have arrived.

Attuned to Terra.name: Attuned to Terra
Attuned to Terra.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Terra, earth and stone—a second, lighter kinship. You can steady a footing, sense a tremor through the ground, or borrow a moment's bedrock strength, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Terra. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Terra.quirk: Terra answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.

Attuned to Ventus.name: Attuned to Ventus
Attuned to Ventus.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Ventus, wind and sky—a second, lighter kinship. You can quicken a step, carry a word on the breeze, or read the mood of the weather, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Ventus. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Ventus.quirk: Ventus answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.

Attuned to Ignis.name: Attuned to Ignis
Attuned to Ignis.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Ignis, flame and heat—a second, lighter kinship. You can bank a fire, warm cold hands, or lend a spark of heat to steel or blood, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Ignis. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Ignis.quirk: Ignis answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.

Attuned to Aqua.name: Attuned to Aqua
Attuned to Aqua.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Aqua, water and tide—a second, lighter kinship. You can draw clean water, still a fever, or catch the truth of a face in an unguarded moment, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Aqua. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Aqua.quirk: Aqua answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.

Attuned to Ferrum.name: Attuned to Ferrum
Attuned to Ferrum.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Ferrum, iron and steel—a second, lighter kinship. You can keep an edge, steady a shield-arm, or blunt a little pain and fear, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Ferrum. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Ferrum.quirk: Ferrum answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.

Attuned to Silva.name: Attuned to Silva
Attuned to Silva.description: Beyond your strongest bond you keep a working attunement to Silva, wood and wild growth—a second, lighter kinship. You can coax a stubborn field, move quiet through green places, or sense the life of the land, though never with the depth of one wholly bonded to Silva. It is the mark of a broad crafter, capable in more than one numen.
Attuned to Silva.quirk: Silva answers you as a second voice—quieter than your primary bond, but always there. Its element is never wholly strange under your hand, and in a pinch you can reach for it as readily as for your first fury.
